job_title: Business Intelligence Engineer, CreativeX Advertising Finance
strengths: Hands-on Python and Pandas for pipelines
Experience with AWS S3 and cloud data
Quantified impact on reporting and operations
weaknesses: Limited direct Advertising/Gen AI domain experience
Under 3+ years in some BI-specific roles
improvement_bullets: Optimized data infrastructure by building automated ETL pipelines in Python, utilizing AWS S3 and Athena to query and transform large-scale datasets, similar to Redshift environments.
Developed high-impact Business Reviews and executive performance monitoring reports using Tableau and Power BI, effectively replacing manual tracking with automated, real-time dashboards.
Conducted deep-dive quantitative analysis on complex datasets, utilizing SQL window functions and NoSQL-style query optimization to identify key performance metrics and business growth opportunities.
Led metrics development initiatives by translating raw operational data into actionable business reviews, providing stakeholders with clear performance benchmarks and visibility into key KPIs.
Managed cloud-based data storage and retrieval workflows using S3, ensuring data integrity and accessibility for cross-functional reporting and advanced analytics projects.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Business Intelligence Engineer, CreativeX Advertising Finance opportunity with Amazon.com. I am interested in this opportunity because of Amazon’s pioneering work in Gen AI and its commitment to using massive-scale data to drive meaningful advertising solutions. What interests me about working for Amazon is its 'Customer Obsession' philosophy and its ability to innovate rapidly within complex ecosystems, which inspires me as a data professional dedicated to building tools that turn ambiguity into clear, actionable strategy. I believe my expertise in architecting end-to-end data pipelines and my proficiency in advanced SQL and Python modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached complex data problems by focusing on scalable automation and high-fidelity reporting. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in manual reporting and developed an automated Power BI dashboard that processed complex datasets, which reduced data processing time by 40%. This project ultimately enabled leadership to make faster, data-driven decisions that contributed to a 300% increase in customer acquisition. I know that the Business Intelligence Engineer role at Amazon would allow me to bring my data engineering and visualization skills to the crucial task of automating Business Review decks and monitoring performance metrics for the Advertising Gen AI team. Additionally, my role as a Data Analytics Fellow at COOP Careers enabled me to develop my deep-dive analysis skills. I designed an end-to-end ETL pipeline for a 2M+ row loan portfolio, utilizing PostgreSQL to clean and standardize complex raw data. This work resulted in the identification of high-risk borrower segments that optimized lending strategies. I am excited by the opportunity to use my background in Python scripting and cloud-based data infrastructure in working on performance monitoring and deep-dive analyses at Amazon. I know that my technical versatility and analytical mindset align with what it takes to succeed at the Business Intelligence Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
